name: Sant Pere de Aüira Trail
name_original: Serra de Montgrony – circular desde Campdevànol al Santuario de Montgrony
description: This 17.6-mile mountain biking trail in Serra de Montgrony offers challenging climbs, panoramic views, and historical landmarks.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Campdevànol offers diverse landscapes in the Catalan Pyrenees, situated at 720 meters elevation and surrounded by mountains exceeding 2,000 meters. The region features beautiful Scots pine and oak woods, providing scenic routes and a natural environment. Varied terrain includes technical single tracks and wider gravel paths, catering to different skill levels. The area is also home to natural features like the Torrent de la Cabana waterfalls and the Coll de Merolla mountain pass. Best mountain bike trails around Campdevànol The most popular mountain bike trail is Serra de Montgrony – circular desde Campdevànol al Santuario de Montgrony, a 17.6 miles (28.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tregurà Mountain Hut – Pla d'Enola Refuge loop from Campdevànol, a difficult 40.2 miles (64.8 km) path. This challenging route features substantial elevation gain, leading through high mountain landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Cafè Cuetes, Ripoll loop from Campdevànol, a 19.3 miles (31.0 km) trail leading through the Ripoll region, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Mountain biking around Campdevànol is defined by Scots pine and oak woods, mountain passes, and technical single tracks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from demanding routes to more basic options. The routes in Campdevànol are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Campdevànol's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Campdevànol?
A: Campdevànol offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 30 routes available. These range from easy to difficult, ensuring options for various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Campdevànol?
A: Yes, Campdevànol has trails suitable for beginners and families. While many routes offer significant challenges, there are 4 easy routes and 16 moderate routes that provide less demanding options. These often feature wider gravel paths through scenic woods.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Campdevànol?
A: The terrain around Campdevànol is highly varied, reflecting its position in the Catalan Pyrenees. You'll encounter beautiful Scots pine and oak woods, technical single tracks, and wider gravel paths. The routes often feature significant elevation changes as they navigate mountains exceeding 2,000 meters.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Campdevànol?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Campdevànol are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936538610/serra-de-montgrony-circular-desde-campdevanol-al-santuario-de-montgrony" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Montgrony – circular desde Campdevànol al Santuario de Montgrony&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop, and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37628625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tregurà Mountain Hut – Pla d'Enola Refuge loop from Campdevànol&lt;/a&gt; also forms a circuit.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails often pass by significant natural features and landmarks. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2524486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrent de la Cabana Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, ride over the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/578909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Merolla&lt;/a&gt; mountain pass, or even catch glimpses of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2097175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taga Summit (2,040 m)&lt;/a&gt;. The region is rich in scenic beauty.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Campdevànol?
A: The mountain bike trails in Campdevànol are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 15 reviews. Over 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the mix of challenging and scenic routes.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 16 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37628625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tregurà Mountain Hut – Pla d'Enola Refuge loop from Campdevànol&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 64.8 km path with nearly 2,000 meters of elevation gain through high mountain landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that lead to viewpoints or high mountain areas?
A: Yes, many routes in Campdevànol ascend to higher elevations, offering panoramic views. The region is surrounded by mountains exceeding 2,000 meters, and trails often lead to mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/578909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Merolla&lt;/a&gt; or provide vistas towards peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2097175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taga Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is a good moderate mountain bike trail to try in Campdevànol?
A: A great moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936538610/serra-de-montgrony-circular-desde-campdevanol-al-santuario-de-montgrony" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Montgrony – circular desde Campdevànol al Santuario de Montgrony&lt;/a&gt;. This 28.3 km trail takes about 3 hours and features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain, offering a rewarding experience without being overly technical.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Ripoll region from Campdevànol?
A: Yes, you can find routes that venture into the nearby Ripoll region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31989200" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Llances – Cafè Cuetes, Ripoll loop from Campdevànol&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 35 km trail that takes you through this area, offering a different perspective of the local landscape.

Q: What kind of natural environment defines the mountain biking experience in Campdevànol?
A: The mountain biking experience in Campdevànol is defined by its stunning natural environment. You'll ride through extensive Scots pine and oak woods, alongside refreshing torrents like the Torrent de la Cabana, and across varied landscapes that showcase the plant diversity of the Catalan Pyrenees.
tips: Good views from the Serra del Montgony.

It is located on the cliffs of the Sierra de Sant Pere, about 1370 meters.
There is also a restaurant.

Romanesque work of the eleventh century.
This monument is one of the first architectural works of the Romanesque period built in Catalonia, and stands out for its immaculate state of the bell tower, which is preserved in very good condition. However, its interior has suffered over the years and several wars, which notably destroyed its walls in the 17th century, or the Civil War, where part of the temple was burned.
In 1990 it was restored and preserved as one of the most outstanding architectural monuments of the Romanesque period in Catalonia.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Sant Pere de Aüira Trail for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as High difficulty for mountain biking. It involves significant climbing and descending, with a positive slope of 410 meters, and includes technically challenging sections, especially during descents. It is best suited for experienced riders.

faqs.question: Is the Sant Pere de Aüira Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, due to its high difficulty rating, significant elevation changes, and technically challenging sections, this trail is not recommended for beginners. It is best suited for experienced mountain bikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Sant Pere de Aüira Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a varied terrain, including simple paths, trails, and gravel roads. You will navigate through forest paths and 4x4 tracks. Some sections can be pronounced and potentially muddy, with technically demanding descents.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable points of interest and panoramic viewpoints. Key highlights include the 12th-century Romanesque Sanctuary of Mare de Déu de Montgrony and the Sant Llorenç de Campdevànol Hermitage. You'll also pass the Pla de Pruners Viewpoint and ride near the Cliffs Towards Collada de Grats. The Romanesque hermitage of Sant Pere d'Aüira, at 1,100 meters, provides sweeping vistas of the Taga river, Sant Marc sierra, Els Rasos de Tubau, and the Freser river valley.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the Sant Pere de Aüira Trail?
faqs.answer: The Sant Pere de Aüira Trail is a circular route that begins and ends in Campdevànol. You can typically find parking in or around Campdevànol to access the trailhead. Specific parking areas may vary, so it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Sant Pere de Aüira Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific regulations regarding dogs are not explicitly stated. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other trail users. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike the Sant Pere de Aüira Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather conditions for mountain biking, with cooler temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot, and winter might bring colder temperatures or snow at higher elevations, potentially making some sections more challenging or inaccessible.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Sant Pere de Aüira Trail or Serra de Montgrony?
faqs.answer: The Sant Pere de Aüira Trail is integrated into the El Ripollès MTB Centre network, indicating it's a recognized route. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trail itself or the broader Serra de Montgrony area for mountain biking. However, always check for any local regulations or temporary restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of other trails. Notably, it covers significant portions of the Etapa 27 and the Sant Pere de Aüria trail itself. You may also encounter shorter segments of El Camí IT1TR3 El Camí Capçaler: de Salzes a Balaguer and La Volta a peu al Ripollés.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the trail starts and ends in Campdevànol, you can find various amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the town before or after your ride. There are no specific amenities directly on the trail itself, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: What should I bring for mountain biking on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length, elevation, and technical sections, it's crucial to bring appropriate gear. This includes a well-maintained mountain bike, helmet, sufficient water, snacks, a repair kit, first-aid supplies, navigation tools (like the komoot app), and weather-appropriate clothing. Due to the challenging descents, protective gear is also highly recommended.
